name: removed_value_persists
code:
proof fn phi_1_removed_value_persists<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len == 1,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
        ret == v,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
    ensures
        post_self@.contains(ret),
{
}
reason: After removing the sole element from a single-element list, the removed value should not still be present in the result

name: nonempty_after_sole_remove
code:
proof fn phi_2_nonempty_after_sole_remove<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len == 1,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
        ret == v,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
    ensures
        post_self@.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: Removing the only element from a length-1 list must yield an empty sequence; a non-empty result indicates a spec inconsistency

name: prestate_length_exceeds_one
code:
proof fn phi_3_prestate_length_exceeds_one<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len == 1,
    ensures
        old_self@.len() > 1,
{
}
reason: Under wf(), value_list_len == 1 must imply the abstract view has exactly 1 element; proving len > 1 would expose a wf/spec_seq_wf inconsistency

name: ret_differs_from_ghost
code:
proof fn phi_4_ret_differs_from_ghost<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len == 1,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
        ret == v,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
    ensures
        ret != v,
{
}
reason: The ensures guarantees ret == v; if ret != v is also provable the entire contract is contradictory (unsatisfiable pre+post)

name: remove_no_effect
code:
proof fn phi_5_remove_no_effect<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    post_self: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    remove_index: SLLIndex,
    v: T,
    ret: T,
)
    requires
        old_self.wf(),
        old_self@.contains(v),
        old_self.get_node_ref(v) == remove_index,
        old_self.value_list_len == 1,
        post_self.wf(),
        post_self.spec_len() == old_self.spec_len() - 1,
        ret == v,
        post_self.unique(),
        post_self@ =~= old_self@.remove_value(ret),
    ensures
        post_self@ =~= old_self@,
{
}
reason: The removal must change the abstract sequence; if post equals pre the spec allows a no-op remove violating the length decrease guarantee
